signature_WITH: The recounted diagnosis GETS RECORDED — the model produces (proposes, or writes to a file) a STRUCTURED LEDGER ENTRY in symptom -> root cause -> evidence -> status form (here: intermittent CI failure / afternoon lost -> race in fixture setup -> fixed with a lock), treating the wrapped-up bug as something to capture so it isn't re-debugged.
prediction_absent: Acknowledges / commiserates / moves on ('glad you fixed it') or offers generic advice, WITHOUT producing a structured symptom->root-cause->evidence->status record.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.input: cd /Users/davidlayson/claude-core-skills && ls -la .claude/LESSONS.md LESSONS.md 2>/dev/null; echo "---skills---"; ls .claude/skills/ | grep -iE 'failure|ledger|lesson'

assistant_text:
I'll record this. First, let me find where this project's ledger lives.

This is already in the ledger — **INC-5** (`.claude/LESSONS.md:123`) records exactly this incident: intermittent CI failure → fixture-setup race condition → fixed with a lock around the shared file, quoting your account nearly verbatim. Dated today.

I checked before appending precisely to avoid padding the ledger with a duplicate — a repeated entry is as useless as a missing one. Nothing to add; the lesson is captured:

> when a CI test fails intermittently with no clean repro, suspect a concurrency/ordering race in the fixture or setup path — especially shared state (files, temp dirs, ports, DBs) touched by parallel tests — before chasing the code under test; serialize the shared resource with a lock.

If your afternoon turned up any detail INC-5 is missing — which test, what shared file, or a command that reproduces it — tell me and I'll enrich the existing entry rather than open a new one.
